Figure 6: Visual comparison of the state space complexity for the Combination Lock problem.

Figure 3: Comparison of action-sequence-based models and our Markovian Models. For action-sequence models, the new action (ah) is appended to the existing action history and fed back into the model as the input for the subsequent prediction. For Markov models, the next action ah is combined with the current state sh and passed through a state transition function P to obtain the next state sh+1, which is the input for the subsequent prediction.
